title: Payment service returning HTTP 503 errors under load
description: The payments-service pods are intermittently returning HTTP 503 Service Unavailable errors. Issue started at 14:32 UTC. Approximately 12% of payment requests are failing. Pod restarts observed in the Kubernetes dashboard. Database connection errors visible in pod logs.
error_message: HikariPool-1 - Connection is not available, request timed out after 30000ms. [payments-db:5432]
resolution_notes: Identified connection pool exhaustion caused by a slow query introduced in the v2.3.1 deployment. Rolled back to v2.3.0. Added index on payments.created_at column. Pool size increased from 10 to 25.
root_cause: Database connection pool exhaustion due to slow query regression

title: Kubernetes pods in CrashLoopBackOff — OOMKilled
description: The recommendation-engine pods are entering CrashLoopBackOff state. kubectl describe shows OOMKilled as the termination reason. Memory usage spiked after the ML model reload at 03:00 UTC scheduled job.
error_message: OOMKilled: Container recommendation-engine exceeded memory limit of 2Gi
resolution_notes: Increased memory limits from 2Gi to 4Gi in the deployment manifest. Added memory-based HPA scaling. Root cause was the new transformer model requiring 3.2Gi at inference time.
root_cause: Kubernetes OOMKilled due to insufficient memory limits for ML model

title: Kafka consumer lag growing — order processing delayed
description: Consumer lag on the orders topic has grown from 0 to 450,000 messages over the last 2 hours. Order confirmation emails are delayed. The order-processor service appears healthy but processing throughput dropped 70% after the v1.8.0 deployment.
error_message: Consumer group order-processor-prod lag: 452183 messages on partition 0-7
resolution_notes: The v1.8.0 deployment introduced synchronous external API calls inside the message handler, reducing throughput from 2000 to 600 msg/s. Moved external calls to async processing. Increased consumer group partition count from 8 to 24.
root_cause: Kafka consumer throughput degradation due to synchronous blocking calls in message handler

title: TLS certificate expiry causing service mesh failures
description: Multiple services are returning SSL_ERROR_RX_RECORD_TOO_LONG errors. The api-gateway cannot establish mTLS connections with downstream services. Certificate rotation was disabled 3 months ago during a migration and not re-enabled.
error_message: SSL handshake failed: certificate has expired (notAfter=2024-02-10T00:00:00Z)
resolution_notes: Renewed wildcard certificate via cert-manager. Re-enabled automatic certificate rotation in Istio mesh configuration. Added certificate expiry monitoring alert with 30-day advance warning.
root_cause: TLS certificate expiry in service mesh due to disabled automatic rotation

title: Redis cache eviction causing database overload
description: Database CPU is at 95%. The catalog-service is generating 40x normal query volume. Redis cache hit rate dropped from 94% to 8% after a configuration change set maxmemory-policy to allkeys-lru with a too-small maxmemory value.
error_message: Redis MAXMEMORY limit reached. Cache miss rate: 92%
resolution_notes: Increased Redis maxmemory from 512MB to 4GB. Changed eviction policy to volatile-lru to protect critical session keys. Implemented cache warming script to pre-populate top 10k catalog items.
root_cause: Redis cache eviction due to misconfigured maxmemory causing database overload
